description_long: This is a large, heavily armored combat vehicle resembling a World War II-era German heavy tank, specifically the Tiger II (King Tiger). The vehicle features a long, prominent main cannon mounted on a rotating turret, with a distinctive angular hull and sloped armor for increased protection. The exterior is covered in a green and white winter camouflage pattern, with visible military markings including a black cross and the number '150' on the turret. The tank is equipped with wide, robust tracks for traversing rough terrain, and has dual exhausts at the rear. The hull and turret are constructed from thick, interlocking armor plates, and the vehicle's size and design suggest it is intended for frontline combat operations. The overall build is robust, with functional details such as hatches, periscopes, and mounting points for additional equipment.
description: A large, heavily armored tank with a long cannon, winter camouflage, and German military markings. It features sloped armor, wide tracks, and a robust, angular design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped glacis plate, two prominent hatches, and the start of the main cannon barrel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the full length of the main cannon, turret, tracks, and visible military cross marking.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the dual exhausts, rear armor, and the back of the turret.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the turret number '150', cross insignia, and the full track profile.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One large cannon mounted on the turret; no other visible weapon mounts.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body coverage with thick, sloped steel armor plating on the hull and turret; reinforced front and turret sides are visually prominent.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Green and white irregular winter camouflage with large, organic shapes and a matte finish.
attribute_military_markings: Black cross insignia on both sides of the turret; number '150' on the right side of the turret.
